channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
item.description: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.
item.content:encoded:
Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.

(Image credit: SOPA Images/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Gett)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
item.description: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.
item.content:encoded:
They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.

(Image credit: Vipin)
item.dc:creator: Omkar Khandekar

item.title: Putin meets North Korean foreign minister in diplomatic push
item.description: The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.
item.content:encoded:
The visit by North Korean Foreign Minister Choe Son Hui comes without an obvious occasion, such as a multilateral diplomatic event or state anniversary, and has raised speculation that she may be coordinating a possible visit to Russia by North Korean leader Kim Jong Un.

(Image credit: Alexander Shcherbak)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 3 U.S. personnel killed in Iran conflict. And, Hunter Biden opens up to NPR
item.description: Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.
item.content:encoded:
Three U.S. service members have been killed since Friday amid rising tension between the U.S. and Iran. And, Hunter Biden talks to NPR's Newsmakers about addiction and public scrutiny.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: At least 930 people have died in Congo's Ebola outbreak
item.description: Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.
item.content:encoded:
Congo's health ministry said the deaths were recorded out of the 2,344 cases confirmed as of Saturday. Thirty-seven new deaths were recorded in the previous 24 hours in one of the highest totals.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran launch new strikes after 3 U.S. service members are killed
item.description: The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. attacks were the ninth consecutive day of bombing since peace talks between the two countries collapsed in dispute over a memorandum of understanding focused on the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: Deloitte-run systems denied Medicaid to disabled people. New laws could make it worse
item.description: Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.
item.content:encoded:
Complex computer systems that decide whether people get Medicaid benefits have a history of errors. Now, changes to comply with Trump's tax and domestic policy law are inundating those systems.

(Image credit: Kate Wells for KFF Health News)
item.dc:creator: Rachana Pradhan

item.title: So distressed you can't think straight? Lower the intensity with these 4 skills
item.description: A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.
item.content:encoded:
A clinical psychologist shares effective strategies to quickly de-escalate stress and anxiety. That includes dunking your head into a bowl of ice water to trigger your mammalian dive reflex.

(Image credit: Jesse Finkelstein/Sonya Barlas/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The U.S. carries out a wave of strikes against Iran, as fighting between the two escalates, Congress faces long to-do list as August recess approaches, Andy Burnham becomes U.K.'s 7th prime minister.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. carries out a wave of strikes against Iran, as fighting between the two escalates, Congress faces long to-do list as August recess approaches, Andy Burnham becomes U.K.'s 7th prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes U.K. prime minister
item.description: In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.
item.content:encoded:
In a transition of power presided over by King Charles, Andy Burnham has replaced Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Both are from the center-left Labour Party.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer
